document.title: Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for H&abyz A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.
keyPoints: Applies to all stages of the life cycle of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.
Identifies product classification as IIb and expected service life as approximately 5 years.
Defines risk acceptability using a severity and probability matrix with acceptable and unacceptable regions.
Requires overall residual risk evaluation after risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
Defines production and post-production information collection and review activities.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: Region Applicable national and regional regulations 1 European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Kim, Kihyun
entities.evidence: Prepared by Kim, Kihyun R&D team April 22, 2024

entities.name: Lee, Dong jin
entities.evidence: Reviewed by Lee, Dong jin R&D Manager April 22, 2024

entities.name: Hur Namkyu
entities.evidence: Approved by Hur Namkyu Quality Manager April 22, 2024

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Essential Performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: We define and document the policy for determining criteria for risk acceptability;

concepts.name: Risk calculation method
concepts.evidence: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

concepts.name: Overall residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

concepts.name: Post-production information
concepts.evidence: Post-production Information should be collected through the collection methods such as surveys, customer satisfaction surveys, customer complaints (Internet, telephone, etc.), or articles or news reports.

concepts.name: Adverse event reporting
concepts.evidence: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency

evidence.claim: The document number is A-TCF-5-103.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-103

evidence.claim: The document is Revision 4 dated April 22, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 4 Risk Management Plan Rev. Date April 22, 2024

evidence.claim: Revision 4 added evidence for estimating the probability of occurrence of harm.
evidence.evidenceText: 4 Apr/22/2024 Added the evidence for estimating the probability of occurrence of harm.

evidence.claim: The plan covers A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The product classification stated in the plan is IIb.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW Classification IIb
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Classification

evidence.claim: The expected service life stated in the plan is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Service Life

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The applicable national and regional regulation listed is Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: EN ISO 14971:2019 is listed as a relevant standard for application of risk management to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: The risk management plan applies to all life-cycle stages of the listed models.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The product consists of TFT and CsI scintillator components and converts read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Operating Principle

evidence.claim: The detectors support auto exposure detection technology without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable national or regional regulations and relevant international standards.
evidence.evidenceText: This policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards

evidence.claim: The plan uses a risk level calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 through 4 are acceptable, and risk levels 5 through 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: Overall residual risk is evaluated after risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

evidence.claim: If overall residual risk is acceptable, significant residual risks are disclosed in accompanying documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: If the overall residual risk is judged acceptable, the manufacturer shall inform users of significant residual risks and shall include the necessary information in the accompanying documentation

evidence.claim: Post-production information is collected from internal and external sources.
evidence.evidenceText: The get information method of post-production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

evidence.claim: Internal post-production information examples include PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, and customer satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: External post-production information examples include medical incident reporting, academic papers, expert opinions, similar device information, and law or standard trends.
evidence.evidenceText: External (1) Reporting medical incidents (Recall, the side effects reported cases of domestic and foreign) (2) Related academic papers and expert opinions (3) Information related similar medical device (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards

evidence.claim: The Quality manager performs risk analysis for collected information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: The Quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text lists European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Korean medical device references, and 3EC (NB); FDA is not present in the provided text.
The source text includes imperfect OCR or formatting in some tables, including misplaced personnel qualification text and line breaks.
The document is marked confidential and intended solely for H&abyz Co., Ltd.; downstream wiki use should preserve source access controls.
